source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Overboard Drilling Fluid Discharge

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, East Breaks Block 699

actions_taken: MPD system put into Flow Control mode
MPD pressure control valves shut by MPD operator
Well shut in using BOP upper annular at 05:20 AM
Kick circulated out using Drillers Method
Drilling fluid returns examined by Mud Engineer
event.summary: On November 16, 2022, at 05:11 AM, approximately twelve barrels of Synthetic Based Mud (SBM) were discharged into the Gulf of Mexico from the Diamond Offshore drillship West Vela during exploration drilling operations at 25,000 feet depth. A saltwater kick of fifty-eight barrels occurred below the base of salt, causing the Managed Pressure Drilling (MPD) system Surface Back Pressure to exceed 1,300 psi, triggering Pressure Relief Valves that were lined up to discharge overboard instead of to the flowline. The well was successfully shut in with shut-in drill pipe pressure of 1,600 psi, and the kick was circulated out. The MPD system was not designed to control influxes greater than five barrels.
recommendations: MPD procedures and hole section influx management envelope matrix should include a differential flow threshold to inform the Driller how to respond when using MPD

When using PCVs for MPD, ensure modeling is performed to determine: 1) optimal PCV setpoints with down hole pumps on based on maximum kick intensity; 2) maximum PCV setpoint based on required SBP for connections with down hole pumps off
Set maximum PCV pressure to protect against PRVs from opening based on a maximum flow rate for the selected setpoint
Review FMCA for plugging downstream of the MPD choke to confirm the correct PRV set point should limit system to pressure rating of equipment upstream of the choke

Review the latest version of the flow control software that responds to influxes in step changes and switches the MPD from Flow Control to SBP mode when the high-pressure limit is reached to allow the chokes to open before the PCV limit is reached
key_phrases: MPD system reverts to Manual Mode
well shut in by BOP upper annular
MPD diverter valve lined up overboard

threats.name: Saltwater influx into wellbore during drilling
MPD system pressure exceeding design limits

consequences.name: Discharge of drilling fluid to marine environment

controls.name: MPD System Flow Control Mode
controls.lod_basis: First automated response to influx detection

supporting_text: First, the MPD system was put into Flow Control mode
The MPD system is not designed to control any kick or influx greater than five barrels
Woodside Energy estimates the total influx from the kick was fifty-eight barrels

controls.name: MPD Pressure Relief Valves (PRV) set at 1,300 psi

supporting_text: Next the Surface Back Pressure (SBP) in the MPD system increased to over 1,300 psi exceeding the 1,300 psi PRV setting for the MPD system
MPD PRV's were set at 1,300 psi

controls.name: MPD Pressure Control Valves (Manual Shutdown)
controls.lod_basis: Operator-initiated shutdown following automatic system failure

supporting_text: The MPD pressure control valves were then shut by the MPD operator
the drilling crew responded appropriately to the incident and shut in the well as directed by the onsite well control procedures

controls.name: Blowout Preventer (BOP) Upper Annular
controls.lod_basis: Final barrier to prevent uncontrolled flow from well

supporting_text: the well was shut in by the driller using the Blowout Preventor's (BOP)upper annular
At 05:20 AM the well was completely shut in and the shut-in drill pipe pressure was 1,600 psi

controls.name: MPD Diverter Valve Lineup to Flowline
controls.barrier_role: Route discharged fluids to containment system
controls.lod_basis: Primary containment routing for PRV discharge

supporting_text: The MPD diverter valve was lined up to go overboard as opposed to the flowline as shown in the MPD new technology drilling application
The flow path from the PRV's was diverted overboard
Decision to divert the drilling fluid overboard
Appendix G.1.5 of the New Technology Application Diamond West Vela East Breaks 699 well 001 rev. 01, shows the flow path going to the flowline during a PRV release

controls.name: Well Control Procedures and MPD Plan
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control defining operational limits and responses

supporting_text: the drilling crew responded appropriately to the incident and shut in the well as directed by the onsite well control procedures and according to the MPD plan
The MPD system is not designed to control any kick or influx greater than five barrels. According to the Hoodoo EB 699 MPD Plan
The MPD plan for the well states any influx greater than 5 barrels must be circulated out through the choke manifold
MPD procedures and hole section influx management envelope matrix should include a differential flow threshold to inform the Driller how to respond when using MPD
The kick was successfully circulated out using the Drillers Method
Drilling fluid returns were examined by the Mud Engineer, and the evidence indicated a saltwater kick without hydrocarbons

controls.name: Mud Engineer Monitoring and Analysis
controls.barrier_role: Verify influx composition and guide response
controls.lod_basis: Technical assessment to inform mitigation strategy

supporting_text: Drilling fluid returns were examined by the Mud Engineer, and the evidence indicated a saltwater kick without hydrocarbons

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.

pif_id_mapping.PIF-001: Competence - Crew responded appropriately to well control event
pif_id_mapping.PIF-002: Situational Awareness - Decision to divert overboard contrary to approved procedure
pif_id_mapping.PIF-003: Procedures - Deviation from New Technology Application flowpath routing
pif_id_mapping.PIF-004: Management of Change - Diverter lineup changed from approved configuration without documented justification
pif_id_mapping.PIF-005: Procedures - MPD procedures lacked differential flow threshold guidance

_validation_errors: bowtie -> controls -> 6 -> side: Input should be 'prevention' or 'mitigation'
